gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Every explicit failure from a `std.time` operation (C1 / G2 / RFC-0013 diagnostic shape)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " No sentinel value, no silent zero, no wrap: every failure route surfaces here."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum TimeErr { # [doc = " A duration or instant arithmetic operation overflowed the representable range."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Never** a wrap-around or a saturating clamp — always this error (C1/G2)."] Overflow , # [doc = " A MONOTONIC or WALL clock could not be read (platform unavailability, spec §7-Q3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Carries an optional human-readable reason (RFC-0013 diagnostic aid)."] ClockUnavailable { reason : & 'static str } , # [doc = " A WALL-clock pair difference found a *backward jump* (NTP, leap, DST)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Never** a silent zero or negative span — always this error (C1). Carries the two"] # [doc = " instants as opaque nanosecond values for diagnostics (RFC-0013)."] NonMonotonic { earlier_ns : i128 , later_ns : i128 } , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A signed nanosecond span (C4 / RFC-0001 value-semantic)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Represented as a signed 128-bit nanosecond count — large enough to span many millennia and"] # [doc = " small enough to be exact at nanosecond resolution. Arithmetic is **always checked** (C1/G2):"] # [doc = " an operation that would overflow returns `Err(TimeErr::Overflow)`, never a wrap or clamp."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Guarantee tag: `Exact`** for all pure arithmetic — the computation is exact integer"] # [doc = " arithmetic; fallibility is the never-silent overflow guard, not approximation."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG — representation (§7-Q4)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The signedness and resolution of `Duration` are open questions (spec §7-Q4): is it signed"] # [doc = " (allowing a negative span from `sub`) or unsigned-with-explicit-direction? This implementation"] # [doc = " uses a **signed `i128` nanoseconds** representation as the simplest honest choice — overflow"] # [doc = " is still `Err(Overflow)`, and a negative span is meaningful (e.g., a deadline that has"] # [doc = " passed). Disposition: defer final representation choice to spec ratification; overflow is"] # [doc = " `Err(Overflow)` regardless (C1 holds either way)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq , PartialOrd , Ord , Hash)] pub struct Duration { # [doc = " Signed nanosecond count. `i128` gives ≈ ±292 years at nanosecond resolution."] nanos : i128 , }
gaps.reason: struct `Duration` uses named fields — no record/product-type surface (only a single-ctor positional shape maps to `type_item`)

gaps.snippet: impl Duration { # [doc = " The zero span."] pub const ZERO : Duration = Duration { nanos : 0 } ; # [doc = " The smallest representable (most-negative) span."] pub const MIN : Duration = Duration { nanos : i128 :: MIN } ; # [doc = " The largest representable (most-positive) span."] pub const MAX : Duration = Duration { nanos : i128 :: MAX } ; # [doc = " Construct from a nanosecond count (value-semantic constructor — always exact)."] # [must_use] pub const fn from_nanos (nanos : i128) -> Self { Duration { nanos } } # [doc = " Construct from whole seconds. Returns `Err(Overflow)` if out of range (C1/G2)."] pub fn from_secs (secs : i64) -> Result < Self , TimeErr > { let nanos = (secs as i128) . checked_mul (1_000_000_000) . ok_or (TimeErr :: Overflow) ? ; Ok (Duration { nanos }) } # [doc = " Construct from milliseconds. Returns `Err(Overflow)` if out of range (C1/G2)."] pub fn from_millis (millis : i64) -> Result < Self , TimeErr > { let nanos = (millis as i128) . checked_mul (1_000_000) . ok_or (TimeErr :: Overflow) ? ; Ok (Duration { nanos }) } # [doc = " Construct from microseconds. Returns `Err(Overflow)` if out of range (C1/G2)."] pub fn from_micros (micros : i64) -> Result < Self , TimeErr > { let nanos = (micros as i128) . checked_mul (1_000) . ok_or (TimeErr :: Overflow) ? ; Ok (Duration { nanos }) } # [doc = " The raw nanosecond count (exact, total — the canonical representation)."] # [must_use] pub const fn as_nanos (self) -> i128 { self . nanos } # [doc = " Truncating whole-second count (rounds toward zero)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - [`TimeErr::Overflow`] when the whole-second count falls outside the `i64` range. The"] # [doc = "   nanosecond field is `i128` (up to [`Duration::MAX`]), so the second count can exceed"] # [doc = "   `i64`; it is refused rather than silently truncated (C1/G2)."] pub const fn as_secs_trunc (self) -> Result < i64 , TimeErr > { let secs = self . nanos / 1_000_000_000 ; if secs < i64 :: MIN as i128 || secs > i64 :: MAX as i128 { return Err (TimeErr :: Overflow) ; } Ok (secs as i64) } # [doc = " Whether this span is negative."] # [must_use] pub const fn is_negative (self) -> bool { self . nanos < 0 } # [doc = " Whether this span is zero."] # [must_use] pub const fn is_zero (self) -> bool { self . nanos == 0 } # [doc = " Negate the span. Returns `Err(Overflow)` for `Duration::MIN` (C1/G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Named `checked_neg` rather than `neg` to avoid ambiguity with `std::ops::Neg`"] # [doc = " (which cannot be implemented here because negation is fallible — C1/G2)."] pub fn checked_neg (self) -> Result < Duration , TimeErr > { self . nanos . checked_neg () . map (| n | Duration { nanos : n }) . ok_or (TimeErr :: Overflow) } # [doc = " Absolute value of the span. Returns `Err(Overflow)` for `Duration::MIN` (C1/G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Named `checked_abs` to match the `checked_*` pattern; `i128::MIN.abs()` would overflow."] pub fn checked_abs (self) -> Result < Duration , TimeErr > { self . nanos . checked_abs () . map (| n | Duration { nanos : n }) . ok_or (TimeErr :: Overflow) } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A point on the WALL-CLOCK (civil/UTC time, an entropy source)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Represented as a signed nanosecond count from the Unix epoch (UTC). This is a civil-time"] # [doc = " value: unlike [`MonoInstant`], its clock can step backward (NTP, leap seconds, DST), so"] # [doc = " `diff(WallInstant, WallInstant)` may return `Err(NonMonotonic)`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Guarantee tag: `Declared`** — a wall read declares `{ time, entropy }`. gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A point on the RFC-0008 LOGICAL clock (a deterministic monotonic tick the runtime advances)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The LOGICAL clock is the **only** time source legible to the deterministic fragment (RT2),"] # [doc = " because its value is reproducible under RT2 sequentialization: it is not real-world entropy."] # [doc = " Its advancement is owned by `std.runtime`/`colony` (M-521 over RFC-0008 §4.7 / M-356) — this"] # [doc = " crate exposes only the **typed reading surface** (a `u64` tick supplied by the caller's"] # [doc = " [`ClockSource`])."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Guarantee tag: `Declared`** — it still declares the `time` effect (a read of runtime state,"] # [doc = " not a pure constant), but `time` only — not `entropy`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG — concrete API ownership (§7-Q1 / M-521 / M-356)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The tick shape (per-colony? per-scope?), the read's exact effect classification, and the"] # [doc = " advancement semantics are **all deferred to M-521/M-356**. gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The injectable clock-source surface (C6 / RFC-0014 declared effects)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " All three reads declare their effect via the return type:"] # [doc = " - `mono_now` returns `DeclaredTime<Result<MonoInstant, TimeErr>>` — effect: `time`."] # [doc = " - `wall_now` returns `DeclaredTimeEntropy<Result<WallInstant, TimeErr>>` — effect:"] # [doc = "   `{ time, entropy }`."] # [doc = " - `logical_now` returns `DeclaredTime<LogicalInstant>` — effect: `time` (deterministic)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The effect marker is on the **return type**, not a doc comment — it is inspectable and"] # [doc = " greppable. Production code passes [`SystemClock`]; tests pass [`ManualClock`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG — std-sys floor (§7-Q3 / M-541)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The real OS clock is accessed only inside [`SystemClock`]. When M-541 lands, that"] # [doc = " implementation will migrate to the audited `wild` block in `std-sys`."] pub trait ClockSource { # [doc = " Read the MONOTONIC clock. Declares effect `time` (not entropy)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Guarantee tag: `Declared`** — an ambient, nondeterministic-across-runs read."] # [doc = " Returns `Err(ClockUnavailable)` if the platform MONOTONIC clock is not available."] fn mono_now (& self) -> DeclaredTime < Result < MonoInstant , TimeErr > > ; # [doc = " Read the WALL-CLOCK. Declares effects `{ time, entropy }`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Guarantee tag: `Declared`** — an entropy source (civil time, RT3). gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The `std.time` guarantee matrix (spec §4 / RFC-0016 §4.5)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Encoding obligation** (RFC-0016 §4.5): every row is encoded here as data and asserted in"] # [doc = " the test suite — never prose-only."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Tag justification (VR-5 — downgrade rather than overclaim):"] # [doc = " - **`Exact` rows** are pure integer-span computations: exact, never approximate. Fallibility"] # [doc = "   is the never-silent overflow guard — a result that cannot be represented is `Err(Overflow)`,"] # [doc = "   never a wrap or saturating clamp (C1/G2)."] # [doc = " - **`Declared` rows** are clock reads: inputs from outside the pure fragment. They are"] # [doc = "   `Declared` because the value is asserted/ambient, not computed. They never tag `Exact`,"] # [doc = "   `Proven`, or `Empirical` — those would be overclaims (VR-5, the crux of this module)."] pub const GUARANTEE_MATRIX : & [GuaranteeRow] = & [GuaranteeRow { op : "duration_add" , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , fallibility : "Err(Overflow) — never wrap/clamp" , effects : "none" , explainable : true , } , GuaranteeRow { op : "duration_sub" , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , fallibility : "Err(Overflow) — never wrap/clamp" , effects : "none" , explainable : true , } , GuaranteeRow { op : "duration_scale" , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , fallibility : "Err(Overflow) — never wrap/clamp" , effects : "none" , explainable : true , } , GuaranteeRow { op : "duration_cmp" , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , fallibility : "total" , effects : "none" , explainable : false , } , GuaranteeRow { op : "duration_as_unit" , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , fallibility : "Err(Overflow) on unit=0 or narrowing overflow" , effects : "none" , explainable : true , } , GuaranteeRow { op : "mono_diff" , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , fallibility : "total (same-source, MONOTONIC is never-backward)" , effects : "none" , explainable : false , } , GuaranteeRow { op : "wall_diff" , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , fallibility : "Err(NonMonotonic) on backward jump — never silent zero" , effects : "none" , explainable : true , } , GuaranteeRow { op : "logical_diff" , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , fallibility : "total (LOGICAL is monotonic by construction)" , effects : "none" , explainable : false , } , GuaranteeRow { op : "mono_now" , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Declared , fallibility : "Err(ClockUnavailable)" , effects : "time" , explainable : true , } , GuaranteeRow { op : "wall_now" , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Declared , fallibility : "Err(ClockUnavailable)" , effects : "{ time, entropy }" , explainable : true , } , GuaranteeRow { op : "logical_now" , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Declared , fallibility : "total (deterministic counter, always readable in v0)" , effects : "time" , explainable : true , } ,] ;

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Assert the structural invariants of the guarantee matrix — called from tests."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Discharges the RFC-0016 §4.5 obligation: \"encoded as data, asserted in tests, never"] # [doc = " prose-only.\" Panics with a descriptive message on any violation."] pub fn assert_matrix_invariants () { for row in GUARANTEE_MATRIX { assert ! (! row . op . is_empty () , "matrix row has empty op name") ; if matches ! (row . op , "mono_now" | "wall_now" | "logical_now") { assert_eq ! (row . tag , GuaranteeStrength :: Declared , "op {}: clock reads must be Declared (VR-5, spec §4)" , row . op) ; } if matches ! (row . op , "duration_add" | "duration_sub" | "duration_scale" | "duration_cmp" | "duration_as_unit" | "mono_diff" | "wall_diff" | "logical_diff") { assert_eq ! (row . tag , GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , "op {}: pure arithmetic must be Exact" , row . op) ; } if row . op == "wall_now" { assert ! (row . effects . contains ("entropy") , "wall_now must declare entropy effect (spec §4 — the typed distinction)") ; } if matches ! (row . op , "mono_now" | "logical_now") { assert ! (! row . effects . contains ("entropy") , "op {}: must NOT declare entropy (spec §4 — typed distinction from wall)" , row . op) ; } if row . tag == GuaranteeStrength :: Declared { assert ! (count , 1 , "op '{op}' must appear exactly once in the matrix") ; } } # [doc = " Clock-read rows tag `Declared`; pure-arithmetic rows tag `Exact`. No overclaim."] # [test] fn matrix_clock_rows_are_declared_arithmetic_rows_are_exact () { let clock_ops = ["mono_now" , "wall_now" , "logical_now"] ; let arith_ops = ["duration_add" , "duration_sub" , "duration_scale" , "duration_cmp" , "duration_as_unit" , "mono_diff" , "wall_diff" , "logical_diff" ,] ; for row in GUARANTEE_MATRIX { if clock_ops . contains (& row . op) { assert_eq ! (d , Duration :: ZERO , "wall_diff(a, a) must be ZERO for ns={ns}") ; } } # [doc = " `wall_diff(later, earlier)` with `later < earlier` is `Err(NonMonotonic)` — never"] # [doc = " a silent zero or negative span (C1/G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Mutation witness: return `Ok(ZERO)` → assertion fires."] # [test] fn wall_diff_backward_jump_is_non_monotonic_never_silent () { let earlier = WallInstant :: from_nanos_since_epoch (100) ; let later = WallInstant :: from_nanos_since_epoch (50) ; let result = wall_diff (later , earlier) ; match result { Err (TimeErr :: NonMonotonic { earlier_ns , later_ns , }) => { assert_eq ! (earlier_ns , 100) ; assert_eq ! (later_ns , 50) ; } _ => panic ! ("expected Err(NonMonotonic), got {result:?} — \
                 a backward jump must never be a silent zero") , } } # [doc = " `wall_diff(a, b)` where `a >= b` returns the positive span (not non-monotonic)."] # [test] fn wall_diff_forward_jump_is_positive_span () { let earlier = WallInstant :: from_nanos_since_epoch (50) ; let later = WallInstant :: from_nanos_since_epoch (150) ; let d = wall_diff (later , earlier) . expect ("forward diff must be Ok") ; assert_eq ! (d . as_nanos () , 100) ; } # [doc = " `mono_now` returns the set mono value and the result is `DeclaredTime<Ok(...)>`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Tests: typed effect marker + value round-trip."] # [test] fn manual_clock_mono_now_returns_declared_time () { let mut clock = mk_clock () ; clock . set_mono (MonoInstant :: from_nanos (12_345)) ; let declared = mono_now (& clock) ; let inner = declared . into_inner () . expect ("ManualClock mono_now is always Ok") ; assert_eq ! (inner . as_nanos () , 12_345) ; } # [doc = " `wall_now` returns the set wall value wrapped in `DeclaredTimeEntropy` — the effect"] # [doc = " marker is structural, not conditional on nondeterminism (the test clock is deterministic,"] # [doc = " but the type still carries the effect declaration)."] # [test] fn manual_clock_wall_now_returns_declared_time_entropy () { let mut clock = mk_clock () ; clock . set_wall (WallInstant :: from_nanos_since_epoch (999_999)) ; let declared = wall_now (& clock) ; let inner = declared . into_inner () . expect ("ManualClock wall_now is always Ok") ; assert_eq ! (inner . as_nanos_since_epoch () , 999_999) ; } # [doc = " `logical_now` returns the set logical tick wrapped in `DeclaredTime`."] # [test] fn manual_clock_logical_now_returns_declared_time () { let mut clock = mk_clock () ; clock . set_logical (LogicalInstant :: from_tick (77)) ; let declared = logical_now (& clock) ; let inner = declared . into_inner () ; assert_eq ! (inner . as_tick () , 77) ; } # [doc = " `advance_mono` and `step_logical` move the clock forward monotonically."] # [test] fn manual_clock_advance_moves_forward () { let mut clock = mk_clock () ; clock . set_mono (MonoInstant :: from_nanos (1_000)) ; clock . advance_mono (500) ; let m = clock . mono_now () . into_inner () . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (m . as_nanos () , 1_500 , "advance_mono should add to the current value") ; clock . set_logical (LogicalInstant :: from_tick (10)) ; clock . step_logical () ; let l = clock . logical_now () . into_inner () ; assert_eq ! (l . as_tick () , 11 , "step_logical should increment by 1") ; } # [doc = " Two reads of `mono_now` are non-decreasing on `ManualClock` without explicit advance."] # [test] fn manual_clock_mono_reads_are_stable_without_advance () { let clock = mk_clock () ; let t1 = clock . mono_now () . into_inner () . unwrap () ; let t2 = clock . mono_now () . into_inner () . unwrap () ; assert ! (t2 >= t1 , "mono reads without advance must be non-decreasing") ; } # [doc = " Document that cross-source difference does not exist — the typed distinction is structural."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " There is no `diff(MonoInstant, WallInstant)` function. Attempting to write one would be"] # [doc = " a compile error, not a runtime check. This test documents the *spec §4* guarantee:"] # [doc = " \"cross-source: — (does not exist) / compile-time type error\"."] # [test] fn cross_source_diff_is_compile_time_type_error_documented () { let m1 = MonoInstant :: from_nanos (10) ; let m2 = MonoInstant :: from_nanos (20) ; let w1 = WallInstant :: from_nanos_since_epoch (10) ; let w2 = WallInstant :: from_nanos_since_epoch (20) ; let _ = mono_diff (m2 , m1) ; let _ = wall_diff (w2 , w1) ; } # [doc = " `from_secs` / `from_millis` / `from_micros` round-trip through `as_nanos`."] # [test] fn duration_constructors_round_trip () { let d = Duration :: from_secs (1) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (d . as_nanos () , 1_000_000_000) ; let d = Duration :: from_millis (1) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (d . as_nanos () , 1_000_000) ; let d = Duration :: from_micros (1) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (d . as_nanos () , 1_000) ; } # [doc = " `from_secs` succeeds for `i64::MAX` because `i64::MAX * 1_000_000_000 < i128::MAX`."] # [doc = " The overflow guard exists for when the input type is widened in the future; for now,"] # [doc = " the `i64` parameter range fits in `i128` nanos. This test documents the actual behavior."] # [test] fn duration_from_secs_i64_max_succeeds () { let result = Duration :: from_secs (i64 :: MAX) ; assert ! (result . is_ok () , "from_secs(i64::MAX) must succeed: i64::MAX * 1e9 < i128::MAX") ; assert_eq ! (result . unwrap () . as_nanos () , i64 :: MAX as i128 * 1_000_000_000) ; } # [doc = " `duration_scale` overflow is the primary overflow path for sub-second arithmetic:"] # [doc = " scaling `Duration::MAX` by any factor > 1 overflows (already tested above)."] # [doc = " Also document that `from_secs` with a negative overflow is not reachable with `i64`."] # [test] fn duration_scale_max_by_two_is_overflow () { let result = duration_scale (Duration :: MAX , 2) ; assert_eq ! (result , Err (TimeErr :: Overflow) , "Duration::MAX * 2 must be Err(Overflow) — never a silent wrap (C1/G2)") ; } # [doc = " The system clock reads non-decreasing mono values (non-deterministic by nature, but"] # [doc = " two successive reads should be non-decreasing on any sane platform)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This test exercises the `SystemClock` std-sys placeholder (FLAG §7-Q3)."] # [test] fn system_clock_mono_reads_are_non_decreasing () { let clock = SystemClock ; let t1 = clock . mono_now () . into_inner () . expect ("SystemClock mono_now must succeed") ; let t2 = clock . mono_now () . into_inner () . expect ("SystemClock mono_now must succeed") ; assert ! (t2 >= t1 , "SystemClock mono reads must be non-decreasing: t1={t1:?}, t2={t2:?}") ; } # [doc = " The system clock wall read wraps the value in `DeclaredTimeEntropy`."] # [test] fn system_clock_wall_now_returns_declared_time_entropy () { let clock = SystemClock ; let declared = clock . wall_now () ; let inner = declared . into_inner () . expect ("SystemClock wall_now must succeed on a sane OS") ; assert ! (inner . as_nanos_since_epoch () > 0 , "SystemClock wall_now must return a post-epoch timestamp") ; } # [doc = " `Duration::from_nanos(n).as_nanos() == n` (round-trip)."] # [test] fn duration_from_nanos_round_trip () { let cases = [0i128 , 1 , - 1 , i128 :: MAX , i128 :: MIN] ; for & ns in & cases { let d = Duration :: from_nanos (ns) ; assert_eq ! (d . as_nanos () , ns) ; } } # [doc = " `MonoInstant::from_nanos(n).as_nanos() == n` (round-trip)."] # [test] fn mono_instant_from_nanos_round_trip () { let cases = [0u64 , 1 , u64 :: MAX] ; for & ns in & cases { let m = MonoInstant :: from_nanos (ns) ; assert_eq ! (m . as_nanos () , ns) ; } } # [doc = " `WallInstant::from_nanos_since_epoch(n).as_nanos_since_epoch() == n` (round-trip)."] # [test] fn wall_instant_from_nanos_round_trip () { let cases = [0i128 , 1 , - 1 , i128 :: MAX , i128 :: MIN] ; for & ns in & cases { let w = WallInstant :: from_nanos_since_epoch (ns) ; assert_eq ! (w . as_nanos_since_epoch () , ns) ; } } # [doc = " `LogicalInstant::from_tick(t).as_tick() == t` (round-trip)."] # [test] fn logical_instant_from_tick_round_trip () { let cases = [0u64 , 1 , u64 :: MAX] ; for & t in & cases { let l = LogicalInstant :: from_tick (t) ; assert_eq ! (l . as_tick () , t) ; } } }
